Logging COA Jobs & Client Review Rounds

A step-by-step guide for Cethos PMs / LV coordinators — Admin portal (portal.cethos.com/admin)

Version 2.0  ·  25 June 2026  ·  Doc TRN-COA-001

Use this to record client COA linguistic-validation jobs (Welocalize, TransPerfect, RWS, etc.) and every client review / feedback round in the admin portal, so the full job history is traceable for ISO 17100 and the IQVIA audit.

Golden rule

The portal is the system of record. When a client sends feedback, a revision, or a clinician round by email, it must also be logged on the order (Part B).

Email + ShareFile alone is not enough — auditors need the trail inside the order.

Part A — Create a job (Direct order)

Use this for back-entering or opening a job for an AR / business client (invoice-on-delivery). Each language pair becomes its own order under one shared project.

Before you click Create

Creating the order sends the client an automatic order-confirmation email (there is no toggle to turn this off yet). For back-entries — jobs the client already knows about — check with your manager first so we don’t send an unexpected email.

1. Start a new project

  1. In the left menu open Orders, then click New project (top-right).

2. Choose Direct order

  1. Pick the Direct order card — “Skip quote — invoice on delivery (AR customers)”.

3. Fill in the order

  1. Customer — search and select the client (e.g. Welocalize, Inc.). This unlocks the Project and Client PM fields lower down.
  2. Service type — pick the service: Clinician Review, Cognitive Debriefing, or the relevant LV service.
  3. Language pair — set Source and Target (e.g. English → English (Canada)). Use + Add language pair for more than one — each pair becomes its own order.
  4. Standard delivery — set the promised delivery date/time (required).
  5. Workflow template — pick the matching template (Clinician Review · 3 steps or Cognitive Debriefing · 3 steps). This auto-creates the workflow steps.

4. Admin fields — Project & Client PM

  1. Project — type the client’s project code (e.g. 2603_P1332). If it doesn’t exist yet, click Create now → get PRJ # to mint a new PRJ-… number. One PRJ per client project code.
  2. Client Project Manager — search the client’s directory for the PM on the job (e.g. Consuelo De Urquiza). If they’re not listed, use + Add new project manager.

5. Create the order

  1. Click Create direct order. You land on the new order (ORD-…).
Reminder

For AR/business clients this fires one order-confirmation email to the customer. Confirm with your manager before creating a back-entry.

6. Set the Order Status

  1. Use the Order Status dropdown (top-left) and set it to In Production for active work (it defaults to “Paid” when the total is $0).

7. Add the receivable (when you have the rate + PO)

  1. Open the Finance tab and add the receivable: description, quantity, rate, tax, PO, and client project number. The order total recomputes automatically. Leave it until the PO arrives if it hasn’t yet.

Part B — Log a client review / feedback round

Do this every time the client emails feedback, a revision request, or a clinician feedback round (CLNFBR). Logging does not send any email — it only records what the client sent.

1. Open Client Communications

  1. Open the order and click the Client Communications tab. Every logged round appears here in an append-only list (the order below already shows one logged round).

2. Add the round

  1. Click + Add client email and fill in:
  • Subject — the client email’s subject line.
  • Email date — back-date this to when the client actually sent it (not “now”), so the timeline is accurate.
  • Email body — paste or summarize exactly what the client asked: instrument, what to fix, how many lines / where highlighted, the deadline, and the ShareFile / package link.
  • Attachments — optional (the flagged file, etc.).

3. Save it (append-only)

  1. Click Save communication. The entry joins the append-only log — it can’t be edited or deleted, which is exactly what auditors want to see.

4. (Optional) Draft the vendor brief

  1. Click Generate to draft AI vendor job-instructions from the logged communications. Review and approve it before the vendor sees it.
  2. If there’s a hard turnaround, note the deadline on the relevant workflow step and assign / notify the vendor.

Part C — Subsequent review rounds

Client feedback often comes in multiple rounds (round 1, round 2, post-review, etc.). Handle each one the same way:

  • Each round = a new entry via + Add client email. Never overwrite the previous one — the append-only log preserves the full sequence in order.
  • Label each round clearly in the subject/body (e.g. “Round 2 — remaining 3 lines on the SIGMA SLE”).
  • After a new round, re-Generate the vendor brief so it reflects the latest feedback, then re-approve.
  • When the deliverable goes back to the client and returns again, that’s another round — log it the same way. The workflow step tracks the revision cycles.

Part D — Quick reference

TaskWhere in the portal
New job for an AR/business clientOrders → New project → Direct order
Log a client feedback / review roundOpen the order → Client Communications → + Add client email
Internal-only note (not shown to client/vendor)Open the order → Add note / Staff notes
Actually email the clientOpen the order → Messages panel (right side) — use deliberately
Pricing / PO / receivableOpen the order → Finance tab → add receivable
Draft a vendor brief from the feedbackClient Communications → Generate (review & approve first)
Cautions
  • Client Communications = logging what the client sent (no email goes out). Messages (right panel) = actually emails the client. Don’t confuse them.
  • Always back-date the email date so the timeline is accurate.
  • Creating an order emails AR/business clients automatically (see Part A).
  • One PRJ per client project code; each language pair is its own order.

Screenshots reflect the live admin portal as of 25 June 2026.
